# Architecture Checklist: Profile Settings Screen

**Purpose**: Validate whether the written requirements and planning artifacts clearly define the requested architectural and UI implementation constraints for this feature.
**Created**: 2026-04-23
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [ ] CHK001 Are the required implementation layers explicitly documented as only `data` and `presintation` for this feature? [Gap, Plan §Project Structure]
- [ ] CHK002 Does the plan define what responsibilities belong in the `data` layer versus the `presintation` layer so the two-layer boundary is unambiguous? [Clarity, Gap, Plan §Summary]
- [ ] CHK003 Are state-management requirements explicitly specified to require `Bloc` rather than leaving room for `Cubit` or UI-local state? [Gap, Ambiguity, Plan §Technical Context]
- [ ] CHK004 Are loading-state requirements documented for this UI-only screen, including whether shimmer is required for initial placeholder rendering? [Gap, Spec §FR-008]
- [ ] CHK005 Do the requirements explicitly state that new UI components must follow existing shared project styling and navigation patterns rather than introducing one-off design rules? [Completeness, Spec §Assumptions]

## Requirement Clarity

- [ ] CHK006 Is the phrase "follow the instruction of the project" translated into concrete, reviewable requirements such as theme reuse, naming alignment, and directory placement? [Ambiguity, Gap]
- [ ] CHK007 Is "builder shimmer" clarified in the written requirements so reviewers can tell whether it means shimmer placeholders for the whole page, only the settings list, or only profile header content? [Ambiguity, Gap]
- [ ] CHK008 Is the term "unified" defined with measurable or reviewable criteria in the feature docs rather than left as a subjective design goal? [Clarity, Gap]
- [ ] CHK009 Are the planned widget additions described precisely enough to show they support the requested design without expanding beyond the approved screen scope? [Clarity, Plan §Project Structure]

## Requirement Consistency

- [ ] CHK010 Do the requested two layers (`data` and `presintation`) remain consistent with the current plan, which presently outlines presentation widgets but no `data` directory? [Conflict, Plan §Project Structure]
- [ ] CHK011 Are the no-logic UI requirements in the spec consistent with any expectation to add a `Bloc`, and is the purpose of that `Bloc` clearly bounded to presentation state only? [Consistency, Spec §FR-008, Plan §Constitution Check]
- [ ] CHK012 Do the screen-design requirements stay consistent with the project constitution requirement to source styling from `lib/core/style/` instead of introducing isolated visual tokens? [Consistency, Plan §Constitution Check]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [ ] CHK013 Can compliance with the two-layer architecture be objectively reviewed from the written requirements without relying on team assumptions? [Measurability, Gap]
- [ ] CHK014 Can a reviewer objectively determine from the docs whether `Bloc` is mandatory for this feature and where its state boundaries begin and end? [Measurability, Gap]
- [ ] CHK015 Are there measurable acceptance criteria for unified app presentation, such as reuse of existing bottom navigation and theme primitives? [Acceptance Criteria, Gap, Plan §Summary]
- [ ] CHK016 Is the shimmer requirement expressed in a way that can be objectively verified from the requirements, including where it appears and under which loading conditions? [Measurability, Gap]

## Scenario Coverage

- [ ] CHK017 Are requirements defined for the loading scenario before profile content is displayed, especially if shimmer placeholders are expected? [Coverage, Gap]
- [ ] CHK018 Are requirements defined for the fallback presentation scenario when profile data is missing while still preserving the unified layout? [Coverage, Spec §FR-009]
- [ ] CHK019 Are requirements documented for how presentation state should behave when controls are visible but logic is intentionally out of scope? [Coverage, Spec §FR-008]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [ ] CHK020 Are edge-case requirements defined for shimmer or placeholder behavior when text length, missing avatar data, or long localized labels affect the layout? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases, Gap]
- [ ] CHK021 Do the requirements address whether the `Bloc` state model should distinguish between loading, content, and fallback-only presentation states even without backend logic? [Edge Case, Gap]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [ ] CHK022 Is the assumption that a `data` layer is still required for a presentation-only feature explicitly documented and justified in the plan? [Assumption, Gap]
- [ ] CHK023 Are dependencies on existing project patterns such as shared theme extensions, bottom navigation, and naming conventions documented clearly enough to prevent divergent implementation? [Dependency, Plan §Technical Context]

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [ ] CHK024 Does the current documentation resolve the tension between "don't add anything from yourself" and the existing plan's proposed new helper widgets by stating which additions are allowed as implementation structure versus new feature scope? [Conflict, Ambiguity, Plan §Project Structure]
- [ ] CHK025 Is it explicitly documented whether shimmer, `Bloc`, and a `data` layer are mandatory requirements requested by the stakeholder or merely implementation preferences inferred later? [Traceability, Ambiguity, Gap]

## Notes

- [ ] CHK026 Do the planning docs explicitly state that helper widgets are allowed only when they keep the code clean without expanding feature scope? [Clarity, Plan §Technical Context, Plan §Project Structure]
- [ ] CHK027 Is the "stay in project scope" expectation translated into reviewable constraints for helper widgets, such as no extra rows, flows, or visual systems beyond the approved feature? [Completeness, Gap]
- [ ] CHK028 Is the clean-code expectation documented with a concrete file-size boundary or equivalent maintainability rule rather than left subjective? [Clarity, Gap]
- [ ] CHK029 Can a reviewer objectively determine from the docs whether screen and helper-widget files are expected to stay around 100 lines or less where practical? [Measurability, Gap]

- This checklist is intentionally written as a requirements-quality review gate.
- Several items are expected to surface gaps in the current spec and plan before `/speckit.tasks` or implementation begins.
